X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

ArubaDsm_DsmSchemaGET.description: Distributed Services Module(DSM) configuration. DSM provide services such as stateful firewall and flow logging.

dsm-instance.description: List of Distributed Services Module(DSM) instance.

bridging-mode.description:
Configure Bridging mode for VXLAN.
By default, packets arriving on an L2VNI over a VXLAN tunnel are not forwarded
to another VXLAN tunnel on the same L2VNI. This behavior is known as DENY.
Set the mode to static-evpn to allow communication between static tunnels and dynamic tunnels.
Set the mode to static-all to enable bridging between static tunnels and any other static or dynamic tunnels.

x-enumDescriptions.DENY: Disable traffic bridging between VXLAN tunnels on same L2VNI.
x-enumDescriptions.STATIC_ALL: Enable traffic bridging between all VXLAN tunnels on same L2VNI.
x-enumDescriptions.STATIC_EVPN: Enable traffic bridging between static and dynamic VXLAN tunnels on same L2VNI.

enable-counters.description:
Enable Aggregate counters for VXLAN.
It records aggregate statistics for packets being sent or received on VXLAN.

ip-version-types.description: Configure IPv4 or IPv6 VXLAN type.

loop-protect.description:
Enable loop-protect on VXLAN, to identify and prevent accidental loops in a network.
Loop-protect is applicable only for IPV4 VXLAN.

items.description:
Configure VLANs to be considered for sending loop-protect pdus for a port.
It is applicable only for IPV4 VXLAN.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description:
Configure MAC notify traps for VXLAN, to generate trap notifications for
four different types of dynamic MAC address related events.

x-enumDescriptions.AGED: Notify when a MAC address aged out on VXLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.LEARNED: Notify when a MAC address was learned on VXLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.MOVED: Notify when a MAC address moved from VXLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.REMOVED: Notify when a MAC address was removed from VXLAN.

src-ipv4.description:
Configure IPv4 source address for VXLAN.
IPv4 source address is applicable only for IPv4 VXLAN.
src-ipv4.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

src-ipv6.description:
Configure IPv6 source address for VXLAN.
IPv6 source address is applicable only for IPv6 VXLAN.
src-ipv6.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

udp-port.description: Configure UDP port for VxLAN tunnel.

ArubaInterfaceVxlan_VxlanSchemaGET.description:
Configure VXLAN. Virtual eXtensible Local Access Network (VXLAN)
provides a framework for Overlaying Virtualized Layer 2 Networks
over Layer 3 networks. In CX it creates a VXLAN Interface.

profile.description:
Global profile for VXLAN configuration parameters,
each scope (global, site, site-collection, device, device-collection)
can accept only one profile. This profile creates VXLAN Interface in AOS-CX.
This list is deprecated now, please use the configuration from aruba-interface-vni.yang

description.description: Configure user description for VXLAN profile.

vni.description:
Configure VNI(s) for VXLAN.
A VNI represents a VXLAN segment and extends the broadcast
domain represented by a VLAN. AOS-CX supports asymmetric routing
by default, symmetric routing is enabled by associating the
VNI with a VRF instead of a VLAN.

id.description: Configure Virtual network identifier for VXLAN.

policy-name.description:
Configure Policy for on VNI.
A policy can be applied only if VRF is configured and routing is enabled on the VNI.

symmetric-routing.description:
Enable symmetric routing for VNI.
Routing cannot be enabled on a VNI that is associated with a VLAN.

vlan.description:
Configure VLAN to VNI. Only one VLAN can be associated with VNI.
VLAN to VNI association is not allowed on a VNI that has symmetric routing enabled or is associated with VRF.
vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

vrf.description:
Configure VRF for VNI for symmetric routing.
VNI to VRF association is not allowed on a VNI that is associated with a VLAN.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

ArubaVsxCommon_IslTimers.description: VSX ISL timer configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

dead-interval.description:
Inter-switch link dead interval. The dead interval is the amount of time to wait for
hellos from peer before declaring the peer to be missing. Lesser dead-intervals might cause
ISL flap due to dead-interval expiry during scenarios like redundancy switchover.

hold-time.description:
Inter-switch link hold time interval.
Port is treated as down only when it stays down for the configured hold-time interval.

peer-detect-interval.description:
Inter-switch link peer detect interval. This is the amount of time in seconds that the
device will wait for inter-switch-link interface to link up after a reboot. If the ISL link
does not come up within this time window, the device will declare itself as split from its peer.

ArubaVsxCommon_IslTimersGet.description: VSX ISL timer configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

dead-interval.description:
Inter-switch link dead interval. The dead interval is the amount of time to wait for
hellos from peer before declaring the peer to be missing. Lesser dead-intervals might cause
ISL flap due to dead-interval expiry during scenarios like redundancy switchover.

hold-time.description:
Inter-switch link hold time interval.
Port is treated as down only when it stays down for the configured hold-time interval.

peer-detect-interval.description:
Inter-switch link peer detect interval. This is the amount of time in seconds that the
device will wait for inter-switch-link interface to link up after a reboot. If the ISL link
does not come up within this time window, the device will declare itself as split from its peer.

ArubaVsxCommon_KeepaliveIp.description: VSX keepalive IP configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

ethernet-ifname.description: Ethernet Interface name for which the keepalive is configured.

if-mgmt.description: Management interface name configured as keepalive.

loopback-id.description: Loopback Interface ID for which the keepalive is configured.

peer-ip.description:
IPv4/IPv6 address of the peer device. For PVOS, only IPv4 is supported.
If not configured, keepalive functionality is disabled.
Configure this if keepalive_type is KA_UPSTREAM.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

portchannel-ifname.description: Port channel Interface name for which the keepalive is configured.

source-ip.description:
IPv4/IPv6 address of the device. If not configured, keepalive functionality is
disabled. Configure this if keepalive_type is KA_UPSTREAM.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

vlan-id.description: Vlan Interface id for which the keepalive is configured.
vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ArubaVsxCommon_KeepaliveTimers.description: VSX keepalive timer configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

dead-interval.description:
Keepalive dead time interval. Default is 3 seconds for CX, 5 seconds for PVOS.
3-20 is the supported range for PVOS.

hello-interval.description:
Keepalive hello time interval in seconds fox CX and in milliseconds for PVOS.
Default is 1 second for CX, 1000 milliseconds for PVOS.
400-10000 in milliseconds is the supported range for PVOS.

udp-port.description:
UDP port number of peer device. Default is 7678 for CX and the default for PVOS is 1024.
1024-49151 is the supported range for PVOS.
udp-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

ArubaVsxCommon_KeepaliveTimersGet.description: VSX keepalive timer configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

dead-interval.description:
Keepalive dead time interval. Default is 3 seconds for CX, 5 seconds for PVOS.
3-20 is the supported range for PVOS.

hello-interval.description:
Keepalive hello time interval in seconds fox CX and in milliseconds for PVOS.
Default is 1 second for CX, 1000 milliseconds for PVOS.
400-10000 in milliseconds is the supported range for PVOS.

udp-port.description:
UDP port number of peer device. Default is 7678 for CX and the default for PVOS is 1024.
1024-49151 is the supported range for PVOS.
udp-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

ArubaVsxCommon_VsxCommon.description: VSX common configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

linkup-delay-timer.description:
Number of seconds that multi-chassis LAGs should be held down after the ISL is
considered to be fully established and VSX switches are synchronized. Comes into
effect on new ISL deployments, ISL flaps or full device reboot.

split-recovery-disable.description:
Disables split brain recovery.
By default, split-recovery mode is enabled. When ISL goes out-of-sync and if
Keep-alive is established then secondary vsx lags will be brought down.
Subsequently if Keep-alive also fails it causes split condition. In this case
if split-recovery mode is enabled secondary will make its vsx lags up else
secondary will continue to keep its vsx lags down.

system-mac.description:
System MAC address to be used by L2 control plane protocols.
A pair of VSX switches must have the same system MAC.
If system MAC is not configured, L2 control plane protocols
will use the VSX Primary device MAC address as their
Bridge Identifier. MAC address will be in
[0-9a-fA-F]{2}(:[0-9a-fA-F]{2}){5} format.

ArubaVsxCommon_VsxCommonGet.description: VSX common configuration grouping, applicable to both primary and secondary

linkup-delay-timer.description:
Number of seconds that multi-chassis LAGs should be held down after the ISL is
considered to be fully established and VSX switches are synchronized. Comes into
effect on new ISL deployments, ISL flaps or full device reboot.

split-recovery-disable.description:
Disables split brain recovery.
By default, split-recovery mode is enabled. When ISL goes out-of-sync and if
Keep-alive is established then secondary vsx lags will be brought down.
Subsequently if Keep-alive also fails it causes split condition. In this case
if split-recovery mode is enabled secondary will make its vsx lags up else
secondary will continue to keep its vsx lags down.

system-mac.description:
System MAC address to be used by L2 control plane protocols.
A pair of VSX switches must have the same system MAC.
If system MAC is not configured, L2 control plane protocols
will use the VSX Primary device MAC address as their
Bridge Identifier. MAC address will be in
[0-9a-fA-F]{2}(:[0-9a-fA-F]{2}){5} format.

role.description:
Specifies whether the device will act as primary or secondary for VSX.
VSX operation requires one peer switch to be primary and the other secondary.
If not configured, then the device will not participate in VSX role election.

Specifies whether the device will act as primary or secondary for VSX.
VSX operation requires one peer switch to be primary and the other secondary.
If not configured, then the device will not participate in VSX role election.

ArubaVsxPair_VsxConfigSchemaGET.description: VSX config for primary or secondary device.

interface-lag.description: The portchannel interface to be used as inter-switch link.

items.description: List of L3 VLANs in use by VSX intent configuration that should not be removed while in use.

get.description:
Configure VXLAN. Virtual eXtensible Local Access Network (VXLAN)
provides a framework for Overlaying Virtualized Layer 2 Networks
over Layer 3 networks. In CX it creates a VXLAN Interface.

delete.description:
(Deprecated) Global profile for VXLAN configuration parameters,
each scope (global, site, site-collection, device, device-collection)
can accept only one profile. This profile creates VXLAN Interface in AOS-CX.
This list is deprecated now, please use the configuration from aruba-interface-vni.yang
